title: Attahas Shaktipeeth
tagline: Attahas Shaktipeeth — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: The temple of Attahas, is believed to be one of the 51 Shakta pithas at which the body parts and jewelry of the Hindu goddess Shakti fell to Earth.
overview.description: The temple of Attahas, is believed to be one of the 51 Shakta pithas at which the body parts and jewelry of the Hindu goddess Shakti fell to Earth. The goddess is so large that the lower lip is about 15 to 18 feet wide. The temple is a site of Hindu pilgrimage throughout the year. December is a particularly popular time for visitors to picnic at the site.

topPlaces.name: Bahula Temple
topPlaces.description: Bahula Temple, or Shri Bahula Shaktipeeth, is a Hindu temple situated in the Ketugram at Katwa Subdivision in the Purba Bardhaman district of West Bengal, India. It is one of the 51 Shakti Pithas.

topPlaces.name: Ketugram
topPlaces.description: Ketugram is a village in Ketugram II CD block in Katwa subdivision of East Burdwan district in the state of West Bengal, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Attahas Shaktipeeth?
faq.a: The best time to visit Attahas Shaktipeeth is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Attahas Shaktipeeth?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (126 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (130 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Attahas Shaktipeeth?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Attahas Shaktipeeth at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Attahas Shaktipeeth?
faq.a: Stays in Attahas Shaktipeeth start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Attahas Shaktipeeth?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bahula Temple, Ketugram, Kandra, Bardhaman, Gangatikuri, Duttabarutia.
